entries.sec_note_zh: 核心生成在本地运行,导出脚本大多仅用 Python 标准库(可选 pip 依赖:python-pptx、PyMuPDF、cairosvg/svglib、mammoth 等)。AI 生图为可选:仅在启用时才需要某个 provider 的 API Key(OpenAI/Gemini/通义/智谱/火山引擎等)并向该厂商发送提示词(按量计费)。TTS 旁白默认用免 Key 的 edge-tts,云端音色可选。网页转 Markdown 工具可选用 curl_cffi 模拟浏览器 TLS 指纹,以抓取屏蔽 Python 默认客户端的门户(如微信公众号)。它还会启动本地 Flask SVG 编辑器和可选的本地 Agent Bridge。

entries.name: Web Shader Extractor
entries.author: lixiaolin94
entries.description_en: Extract a webpage's WebGL/WebGPU/Canvas/shader visual effect and rebuild it as a standalone native-JS project. Locks the target rendering surface, gathers shader/resource/render-graph/timing evidence, builds an evidence-matched local baseline, then projectizes it—evidence before guessing, baseline before refactor.
entries.description_zh: 把网页里的 WebGL/WebGPU/Canvas/shader 视觉特效提取出来,重建为独立的原生 JS 项目。先锁定目标渲染面,采集 shader/资源/渲染图/时序等证据,搭出与证据吻合的本地基线,再工程化为可编辑项目——先证据后猜测、先基线后重构。
entries.long_en: One skill from lixiaolin94's collection (the curated entry installs just the web-shader-extractor folder, not the whole repo). A router-style SKILL.md plus a dozen focused references and JSON/markdown templates drive a disciplined recon state machine: intake → surface attribution → target lock → source/runtime trace → minimum-truth capture → verified baseline → projectize → QA. It labels facts as SOURCE / PARTIAL / GUESS, forbids 'compensation tuning' to mask missing pipeline evidence, and ships platform adapters for Three.js/TSL, Unicorn Studio, and shaders.com. Bundled scripts (rendered-DOM inventory, bundle scan) are treated as hypotheses, never as gate-passing proof. Not for ordinary DOM/CSS page cloning.
entries.long_zh: lixiaolin94 技能合集中的一个技能(本条目只安装 web-shader-extractor 这一个目录,而非整仓)。路由式 SKILL.md + 十余篇聚焦参考文档 + JSON/markdown 模板,驱动一套严谨的侦察状态机:摄入 → 渲染面归因 → 目标锁定 → 源码/运行时追踪 → 最小真值采集 → 基线校验 → 工程化 → QA。它把事实标注为 SOURCE / PARTIAL / GUESS,禁止用「补偿调参」掩盖缺失的管线证据,并内置 Three.js/TSL、Unicorn Studio、shaders.com 平台适配。附带脚本(渲染 DOM 盘点、bundle 扫描)只作假设证据,不能单独通过任何关卡。不用于普通 DOM/CSS 页面克隆。
entries.sec_note_en: Drives a headless browser (e.g. Playwright) to load and capture the target site, and may deobfuscate/inspect the target's JavaScript bundle and source maps to recover shader code. It is meant for studying and porting visual effects, not bulk site cloning—you are responsible for respecting the source site's copyright, license, and terms when reusing extracted shaders/assets. The repo's README states MIT, but it ships no LICENSE file.
entries.sec_note_zh: 会驱动无头浏览器(如 Playwright)加载并采集目标站点,并可能反混淆/分析目标的 JavaScript bundle 与 source map 以还原 shader 代码。它用于研究与移植视觉特效,而非批量克隆站点——复用提取到的 shader/资源时,你需自行尊重源站的版权、许可与条款。仓库 README 标注 MIT,但未附 LICENSE 文件。

entries.name: Weekly Report
entries.author: lixiaolin94
entries.description_en: Generate a send-ready weekly report by collecting evidence across four channel families—collaboration platforms, Git history, agent sessions, and local docs—normalizing it, merging into work topics, and writing a structured report that separates facts from inference. Resolves an absolute date range before collecting.
entries.description_zh: 通过四类通道——协作平台、Git 历史、Agent 会话、本地文档——采集证据,归一化后并入「工作主题」,生成可直接发送、事实与推断分开的结构化周报。采集前先解析出绝对日期范围。
entries.long_en: One skill from lixiaolin94's collection (the curated entry installs just the weekly-report folder, not the whole repo). It runs a disciplined pipeline: resolve the week's absolute range (via a bundled `resolve_week_range.py`), discover which channels are available (collab_platform, git, agent, local_docs), collect each channel's capabilities into normalized Evidence objects (with source, actors, timestamp, url/path, confidence), merge them into Work Topics (what/status/key conclusions/supporting evidence/next-week), and render a report from a template. Rules enforce a stated date range, ≥2 evidence types per core item (or an explicit 'insufficient evidence' flag), and a hard split between fact and inference. References cover each channel (incl. Lark/Feishu, Git, local docs).
entries.long_zh: lixiaolin94 技能合集中的一个技能(本条目只安装 weekly-report 这一个目录,而非整仓)。它运行一套严谨的流水线:先解析本周的绝对日期范围(用附带的 `resolve_week_range.py`),发现当前可用通道(collab_platform、git、agent、local_docs),把各通道能力采集为归一化的 Evidence 对象(含来源、参与者、时间戳、url/路径、置信度),再并入「工作主题」(是什么/状态/关键结论/支撑证据/下周是否推进),最后按模板渲染周报。规则强制:明确日期范围、每项核心工作至少两类证据(或显式标注「证据不足」)、事实与推断硬性分开。参考文档覆盖各通道(含飞书/Lark、Git、本地文档)。
entries.sec_note_en: Reads from whatever evidence channels you enable—Git history (local), agent session logs, local documents, and collaboration platforms (e.g. Lark/Feishu via their APIs). The collaboration-platform channels need your own credentials/tokens and pull work content (calendars, meeting notes, messages) into the report context; review what gets collected, and the report before sending. Runs a bundled Python script (`resolve_week_range.py`) for date math.
entries.sec_note_zh: 从你启用的证据通道读取——Git 历史(本地)、Agent 会话记录、本地文档,以及协作平台(如通过飞书/Lark 的 API)。协作平台通道需要你自己的凭据/令牌,会把工作内容(日历、会议纪要、消息)拉进周报上下文;请审阅采集到的内容,以及发送前的周报。日期计算会运行附带的 Python 脚本(`resolve_week_range.py`)。

entries.name: Obsidian Skills
entries.author: kepano
entries.description_en: Five skills that teach an agent to work with an Obsidian vault's open formats—Obsidian Flavored Markdown (wikilinks, callouts, embeds, properties), Bases (.base) database views, JSON Canvas (.canvas), the Obsidian CLI, and Defuddle web-to-clean-markdown. By Obsidian's own maker, Steph Ango (kepano).
entries.description_zh: 五个技能,教 agent 操作 Obsidian 仓库的开放格式——Obsidian 风格 Markdown(wikilink、callout、嵌入、属性)、Bases(.base)数据库视图、JSON Canvas(.canvas)、Obsidian CLI,以及 Defuddle 网页转干净 Markdown。出自 Obsidian 作者本人 Steph Ango(kepano)。
entries.long_en: A Claude Code plugin (also Agent-Skills-spec compatible, so it works in Codex and OpenCode) bundling obsidian-markdown, obsidian-bases, json-canvas, obsidian-cli, and defuddle. Each is invocable on demand: create/edit notes with Obsidian-specific syntax, build database-like Bases views with filters and formulas, lay out visual canvases, drive the vault from the CLI (including plugin/theme dev—reload plugins, run JS, capture errors, screenshot, inspect the DOM), and extract clutter-free markdown from web pages to save tokens. The formats are open specs, so output stays portable.
entries.long_zh: 一个 Claude Code 插件(同时兼容 Agent-Skills 规范,可用于 Codex 与 OpenCode),打包 obsidian-markdown、obsidian-bases、json-canvas、obsidian-cli、defuddle 五个技能。可按需调用:用 Obsidian 专有语法创建/编辑笔记,用 Bases 建带筛选与公式的类数据库视图,排布可视化 canvas,从命令行驱动仓库(含插件/主题开发——重载插件、运行 JS、捕获错误、截图、检查 DOM),以及把网页提取为去杂的 markdown 以省 token。所用格式均为开放规范,产物可移植。
entries.sec_note_en: MIT-licensed; operates locally on your Obsidian vault files. obsidian-cli can run arbitrary JavaScript and mutate vault contents (note/task/property operations, plugin/theme debugging)—review actions before running on a real vault. defuddle fetches and cleans external web pages you point it at. The skills themselves send no vault data anywhere.
entries.sec_note_zh: MIT 许可;在本地 Obsidian 仓库文件上操作。obsidian-cli 可运行任意 JavaScript 并改动仓库内容(笔记/任务/属性操作、插件/主题调试)——在真实仓库上执行前请先审阅动作。defuddle 会抓取并清洗你指定的外部网页。技能本身不会把仓库数据发往任何地方。

entries.name: Understand Anything
entries.author: Egonex-AI
entries.description_en: A multi-agent Claude Code plugin that analyzes any codebase into an interactive knowledge graph—every file, function, class, and dependency as a clickable node with plain-English summaries—plus guided tours, fuzzy/semantic search, diff impact analysis, architectural layer grouping, and a business-domain view.
entries.description_zh: 一个多 agent 的 Claude Code 插件,把任意代码库解析成可交互的知识图谱——每个文件、函数、类、依赖都是可点击节点并带白话摘要——并提供引导式导览、模糊/语义搜索、diff 影响分析、架构分层归类与业务域视图。
entries.long_en: Runs a multi-agent pipeline (project scan → per-file analysis → architecture layering → domain extraction → graph assembly → review) that emits JSON graphs and an interactive dashboard you pan, zoom, and search in the browser. Skills include /understand (build the graph), /understand-dashboard, /understand-explain, /understand-onboard, /understand-diff (ripple effect of a change), /understand-domain (business flows), /understand-knowledge (turn a Karpathy-style LLM wiki into a force-directed, community-clustered graph), and /understand-chat. Cross-platform: Claude Code marketplace, plus Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI and others via the repo's install.sh.
entries.long_zh: 运行一条多 agent 流水线(项目扫描 → 逐文件分析 → 架构分层 → 业务域抽取 → 图谱组装 → 审校),产出 JSON 图谱与可在浏览器中平移、缩放、搜索的交互式仪表盘。技能包括 /understand(建图)、/understand-dashboard、/understand-explain、/understand-onboard、/understand-diff(改动的连锁影响)、/understand-domain(业务流)、/understand-knowledge(把 Karpathy 式 LLM wiki 变成力导向、社区聚类的图谱)与 /understand-chat。跨平台:Claude Code 市场,外加 Codex、Cursor、Copilot、Gemini CLI 等可经仓库的 install.sh 安装。
entries.sec_note_en: MIT-licensed; the analysis runs locally through your own agent/LLM—your source is read in bulk and fed to the model, so expect significant token usage on large repos and avoid pointing it at secrets-laden trees you don't want sent to your model provider. Output (graphs + dashboard) is written locally; an optional hosted demo/homepage exists but isn't required to use the plugin.
entries.sec_note_zh: MIT 许可;分析通过你自己的 agent/LLM 在本地运行——会大量读取源码并喂给模型,因此大仓库会产生可观的 token 消耗,也不宜对着含密钥、你不愿发送给模型厂商的目录使用。产物(图谱+仪表盘)写在本地;存在可选的托管 demo/主页,但使用插件并不依赖它。

entries.name: Paper Analyzer
entries.author: zsyggg
entries.description_en: Turn an academic paper (arXiv link, PDF, or pasted text) into a polished, shareable HTML deep-dive article. Runs a mandatory multi-round workflow: read the full paper, search GitHub for the matching code and cross-reference it, then write in one of three styles—storytelling / academic / concise—with KaTeX formulas and Mermaid diagrams. Zero config, no API keys.
entries.description_zh: 把学术论文(arXiv 链接、PDF 或粘贴文本)转成精美、可直接分享的 HTML 深度解读长文。运行强制多轮工作流:读全文 → 搜 GitHub 找对应代码并交叉引用 → 按三种风格之一(故事 / 学术 / 速览)写作,带 KaTeX 公式与 Mermaid 图表。零配置、无需 API key。
entries.long_en: One of three skills in zsyggg's paper-craft collection (this entry installs just the paper-analyzer folder, not the whole repo). It runs a disciplined 6-round pipeline that cannot be skipped: (1) fetch the full paper—reading both the arXiv abstract and HTML full text, or the PDF, or pasted text; (2) find and clone the matching open-source code (`git clone --depth 1` into /tmp) and locate ≥2 concrete paper↔source correspondences; (3) analyze core contributions, method, key experiments, and weaknesses internally; (4) ask the user for a writing style; (5) emit a complete standalone HTML article; (6) self-review against per-style hard requirements. Three styles—storytelling (viral-blog voice), academic (peer-review-grade, ≥5 KaTeX formulas and comparison tables), concise (cheat-sheet with a Mermaid diagram). Bundled Python scripts handle paper-info extraction and HTML generation.
entries.long_zh: zsyggg paper-craft 三件套技能之一(本条目只安装 paper-analyzer 这一个目录,而非整仓)。它运行一套不可跳过的 6 轮流水线:(1) 获取论文全文——同时读 arXiv 摘要页与 HTML 全文,或读 PDF,或用粘贴文本;(2) 找到并克隆对应开源代码(`git clone --depth 1` 到 /tmp),定位 ≥2 处论文↔源码对应;(3) 内部分析核心创新、方法、关键实验与弱点;(4) 询问用户写作风格;(5) 输出完整独立 HTML 长文;(6) 按各风格硬标准自我审查。三种风格——故事型(公众号爆文腔)、学术型(同行评审级,≥5 处 KaTeX 公式 + 对比表)、速览型(带 Mermaid 图的速查卡)。附带 Python 脚本负责论文信息抽取与 HTML 生成。
entries.sec_note_en: Reaches the network: fetches arXiv pages/PDFs and, to cross-reference code, searches GitHub and runs `git clone --depth 1` on the repository it finds into /tmp. Reads local PDFs you point it at and runs bundled Python scripts (extract_paper_info.py, generate_html.py) to parse the paper and emit the HTML. No API keys or accounts. Review any cloned third-party code and the generated article before sharing. The repo's README states MIT, but it ships no LICENSE file.
entries.sec_note_zh: 会联网:抓取 arXiv 页面/PDF,并为交叉引用代码而搜索 GitHub、对找到的仓库执行 `git clone --depth 1` 到 /tmp。读取你指定的本地 PDF,并运行附带的 Python 脚本(extract_paper_info.py、generate_html.py)解析论文、生成 HTML。无需 API key 或账号。分享前请审阅克隆的第三方代码与生成的文章。仓库 README 标注 MIT,但未附 LICENSE 文件。

entries.name: Paper Comic
entries.author: zsyggg
entries.description_en: Turn a paper's core method into visual method figures that explain exactly what it does and how. Reads the paper, proposes a cover / overview / mechanism-detail figure plan, and—only after you confirm scope, count, language, and style—generates 1–10 figures in a warm sketchnote or publication-grade paper-figure style.
entries.description_zh: 把论文的核心方法转成讲清楚「做了什么、怎么做」的视觉方法图。读论文后先给出封面/概述/机制细节的出图方案,在你确认范围、张数、语言、风格之后,再生成 1–10 张图,支持温暖手记风(sketchnote)或顶会论文框架图风(paper-figure)。
entries.long_en: One of three skills in zsyggg's paper-craft collection (this entry installs just the paper-comic folder, not the whole repo). Unlike a generic article illustrator, it draws only three things—method flow (input→process→output), the single most novel mechanism unpacked, and the most convincing results—and refuses to decorate or visualize what a sentence already explains. The workflow is confirmation-gated: it reads the paper, recommends a figure plan (cover / overview / mechanism details / key result) with a suggested count and rationale, and will not generate until you confirm language, scope, count, and style. Two styles: sketchnote (bright, warm hand-drawn study-note look) and paper-figure (clean, publication-grade overview diagram). It hardcodes no image API—at runtime it detects and uses whatever image-generation backend is available (e.g. Codex's imagegen or an installed Claude Code image-gen skill).
entries.long_zh: zsyggg paper-craft 三件套技能之一(本条目只安装 paper-comic 这一个目录,而非整仓)。与通用配图工具不同,它只画三样东西——方法流程(输入→处理→输出)、最创新的那个机制(拆开看内部)、最有说服力的结果——并拒绝装饰图或把一句话就能讲清的东西画出来。工作流强制确认:读完论文先给出出图方案(封面 / 概述 / 机制细节 / 关键结果)及建议张数和理由,在你确认语言、范围、张数、风格之前不会生成。两种风格:sketchnote(明亮温暖的手绘研究手记风)与 paper-figure(干净的顶会级方法总览框架图)。它不写死任何图像 API——运行时自动检测并使用当前可用的生图后端(如 Codex 的 imagegen 或已安装的 Claude Code 生图技能)。
entries.sec_note_en: Needs an image-generation backend to produce figures—it hardcodes no API key and instead uses whatever is installed (Codex's built-in imagegen, or a Claude Code image-gen skill); that backend may require its own account/credits and sends your figure prompts to a third-party model. Reads the paper (arXiv/PDF) to plan figures. By design it asks for confirmation before generating, so you control scope and cost. The repo's README states MIT, but it ships no LICENSE file.
entries.sec_note_zh: 生成图片需要一个生图后端——它不写死任何 API key,而是使用已安装的后端(Codex 内置 imagegen,或某个 Claude Code 生图技能);该后端可能需要自己的账号/额度,并会把你的出图 prompt 发送给第三方模型。读论文(arXiv/PDF)以规划出图。设计上生成前会先确认,因此范围与花费由你掌控。仓库 README 标注 MIT,但未附 LICENSE 文件。

entries.name: Paper Deck
entries.author: zsyggg
entries.description_en: Turn a paper, article, or technical note into a designed AIGC slide deck that doesn't look like a template. Builds a deck brief and slide-by-slide outline, writes a reproducible visual prompt per page, generates 16:9 slide images via a raster image backend, and merges them into .pptx and .pdf. Four style presets; can mount real figures cropped from the source PDF.
entries.description_zh: 把论文、技术文章或知识内容做成「看起来真的被设计过」的 AIGC 幻灯片。先做 deck brief 和逐页大纲,为每页写可复现的视觉 prompt,用生图后端生成 16:9 slide image,再合成 .pptx 与 .pdf。四种风格预设;可把源 PDF 里的真实图表裁剪后嵌入版面。
entries.long_en: One of three skills in zsyggg's paper-craft collection (this entry installs just the paper-deck folder, not the whole repo). A raster-first AIGC slide workflow: it analyzes the source into an analysis.md, gets your confirmation, writes a deck-brief.md and a per-page outline.md (one message per slide), then generates each 16:9 page as a real generated image and merges everything into .pptx and .pdf—keeping every page's prompt as a re-editable source so you can ask for precise changes ('redo slide 5', 'make it more journal-like'). Four style presets: journal-minimal, business-research, warm-notes, liquid-glass. It can plan and mount real source visuals (figures, tables, plots cropped from the PDF) instead of hallucinating them. It deliberately refuses to fake 'generated' pages with local drawing (Pillow/SVG/HTML/matplotlib) and stops if no raster image backend is available. A bundled Python script merges the deck.
entries.long_zh: zsyggg paper-craft 三件套技能之一(本条目只安装 paper-deck 这一个目录,而非整仓)。raster-first 的 AIGC 幻灯片工作流:先把源内容分析成 analysis.md,经你确认后写 deck-brief.md 和逐页 outline.md(每页一个观点),再把每一页 16:9 作为真实生图输出,最后合成 .pptx 与 .pdf——并保留每页 prompt 作为可返修源文件,便于你精确返修(「重做第 5 页」「更像期刊风」)。四种风格预设:journal-minimal、business-research、warm-notes、liquid-glass。它能规划并嵌入真实素材(从 PDF 裁剪的图、表、曲线)而非凭空生成。它刻意拒绝用本地绘图(Pillow/SVG/HTML/matplotlib)伪造「生图页」,若没有可用的生图后端会直接停止。附带的 Python 脚本负责合成 deck。
entries.sec_note_en: Requires a raster image-generation backend (it refuses to fall back to local drawing and stops if none is available); that backend may need its own account/credits and receives your per-slide prompts. Reads the source (arXiv/PDF), may screenshot/crop real figures from your PDF, and runs a bundled Python script to merge pages into .pptx/.pdf on disk. It asks for confirmation before generating, so you control page count and cost. The repo's README states MIT, but it ships no LICENSE file.
entries.sec_note_zh: 需要一个生图后端(它拒绝退化为本地绘图,没有可用后端就停止);该后端可能需要自己的账号/额度,并会收到你每页的 prompt。读取源内容(arXiv/PDF),可能从你的 PDF 截图/裁剪真实图表,并运行附带的 Python 脚本把各页合成 .pptx/.pdf 写到磁盘。生成前会先确认,因此页数与花费由你掌控。仓库 README 标注 MIT,但未附 LICENSE 文件。

entries.name: Black White Text Opener
entries.author: Pluviobyte
entries.description_en: Create reusable black-background, white-text opening animations—large text typed in character by character, synchronized typing-click SFX, optional word swaps, and a clean transition into the main video. Original opener creation, not reference matching.
entries.description_zh: 做可复用的黑底白字开场动画——大字逐字打出、同步打字音效、可选词语替换、干净转场切入正片。原创开场生成，非参考复刻。
entries.long_en: One skill from Pluviobyte's video-production-skills collection (this entry installs only the black-white-text-opener folder, not the whole repo). A generative opener skill: it reads a motion contract and timing presets, picks HyperFrames / Remotion / ffmpeg via a tool-recipes reference, and defaults to ~14 chars/s typing with subtle key clicks over a 4.8–5.8s 'standard' duration. Given only a topic it drafts 2–3 short phrase options and picks the cleanest; it stays generative and never claims pixel-level alignment with a source.
entries.long_zh: Pluviobyte 视频制作技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 black-white-text-opener 这一个目录，而非整仓）。生成型开场技能：先读动效契约与时间预设，按 tool-recipes 在 HyperFrames / Remotion / ffmpeg 间选型，默认约 14 字/秒打字、轻微按键音、4.8–5.8 秒「standard」时长。只给主题时会起草 2–3 个短句备选并挑最干净的；始终是生成型，不会宣称与源片像素级对齐。
entries.sec_note_en: Requires HyperFrames / Remotion or ffmpeg to render the animation and typing-click audio; renders locally. No external API or credentials needed.
entries.sec_note_zh: 渲染动画与打字音效需要 HyperFrames / Remotion 或 ffmpeg；本地渲染。无需外部 API 或凭据。

entries.name: Dark SaaS Magic Video
entries.author: Pluviobyte
entries.description_en: Create dark, cinematic SaaS/AI-product motion videos in a Presenton-like 'magic UI' style—black starfield stage, bottom purple glow, large kinetic type, gradient CTA buttons, floating UI templates, model rings, and speed-blur transitions. Style-level creation, not frame-accurate replication.
entries.description_zh: 用类 Presenton 的「魔术 UI」风格做暗色电影感的 SaaS / AI 产品短片——黑色星空舞台、底部紫光、动感大字、渐变 CTA、漂浮 UI 模板、模型环、速度模糊转场。属风格级创作，非逐帧复刻。
entries.long_en: One skill from Pluviobyte's video-production-skills collection (this entry installs only the dark-saas-magic-video folder, not the whole repo). A style-generation skill, not reference-alignment—use reference-video-replica-qc for exact remakes. It reads a style language, scene blueprints, timing presets, and an asset contract before composing, runs a QC checklist before handoff, and builds with HyperFrames by default (Remotion on request). A bundled 1080p showcase sets the bar for scale, pacing, and polish.
entries.long_zh: Pluviobyte 视频制作技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 dark-saas-magic-video 这一个目录，而非整仓）。这是风格生成技能，非参考对齐——逐帧复刻请用 reference-video-replica-qc。它在落地前先读风格语言、场景蓝图、时间预设与素材契约，交付前过质检清单，默认用 HyperFrames 构建（按需 Remotion）。附带的 1080p 成片设定了规模、节奏与完成度的基准。
entries.sec_note_en: Requires a Node-based video rendering toolchain (HyperFrames by default, or Remotion) to produce output; renders locally. The skill itself needs no paid API.
entries.sec_note_zh: 需要基于 Node 的视频渲染工具链（默认 HyperFrames，或 Remotion）来出片；本地渲染。技能本身不需要付费 API。

entries.name: Reference Video Replica QC
entries.author: Pluviobyte
entries.description_en: Make reference-video replication evidence-driven: classify the requested fidelity (pixel / visual / style), sample frames at a set interval, build timelines and side-by-side contact sheets, run PSNR/SSIM/hash checks, then approve or reject with hard evidence.
entries.description_zh: 让参考视频复刻变成证据驱动：先判定保真级别（像素 / 视觉 / 风格），按间隔抽帧、生成时间线与对照图，跑 PSNR/SSIM/哈希校验，再用硬证据决定通过或打回。
entries.long_en: One skill from Pluviobyte's video-production-skills collection (this entry installs only the reference-video-replica-qc folder, not the whole repo). It refuses to claim pixel-level alignment by feel: pixel-level needs a matching SHA-256/`cmp`, PSNR infinity, or SSIM 1.0; visual-level is a hand-authored recreation matching timing, layout, motion, typography, and color; style-level uses the reference only as a design language. Bundled Python scripts (`extract_halfsec_frames.py`, `compare_videos.py`) do 0.5s frame sampling and metric comparison; references cover replica levels, alignment workflow, and lessons learned.
entries.long_zh: Pluviobyte 视频制作技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 reference-video-replica-qc 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它拒绝凭感觉宣称像素级对齐：像素级需一致 SHA-256/`cmp`、PSNR 无穷或 SSIM 1.0；视觉级是手工还原、匹配时间/布局/动效/字体/色彩；风格级只把参考当设计语言。附带的 Python 脚本（`extract_halfsec_frames.py`、`compare_videos.py`）做 0.5 秒抽帧与指标比对；参考文档覆盖保真级别、对齐流程与复刻经验。
entries.sec_note_en: Runs bundled Python scripts that need ffmpeg and image/video libraries (frame extraction, PSNR/SSIM, hashing); may fetch a remote source video for analysis. Review any URL it downloads from.
entries.sec_note_zh: 运行附带的 Python 脚本，需要 ffmpeg 与图像/视频库（抽帧、PSNR/SSIM、哈希）；可能为分析拉取远程源视频。请审阅它下载的来源 URL。

entries.name: Ask Matt
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Ask which skill or flow fits your situation — a router over the user-invoked engineering skills in this repo.
entries.description_zh: 询问哪个技能或工作流最适合你当前的处境——是这个仓库里所有用户调用型工程技能的路由器。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/ask-matt folder, not the whole repo). It acts as a router that, in conversation, maps your situation onto the right skill or flow (the idea-to-ship main flow, on-ramps like triage, and standalone helpers), explaining when to chain grill-with-docs, prototype, to-prd, to-issues, and handoff.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/ask-matt 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它扮演路由器的角色，在对话中把你当前的处境映射到合适的技能或工作流（从想法到交付的主流程、triage 等入口、以及独立辅助技能），讲清何时串联 grill-with-docs、prototype、to-prd、to-issues 与 handoff。
entries.sec_note_en: Pure conversational orchestration: it only reads your description and recommends which other skill to run. No external side effects, no code execution, no file writes of its own. It merely points at other skills (some of which do have side effects) — running those is a separate, explicit step.
entries.sec_note_zh: 纯对话式编排：它只读取你的描述并推荐该运行哪个其他技能。自身没有外部副作用、不执行代码、不写文件。它只是指向其他技能（其中部分技能确实有副作用）——运行那些技能是单独且明确的步骤。

entries.name: Codebase Design
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Shared vocabulary for designing deep modules. Use when designing or improving a module's interface, finding deepening opportunities, deciding where a seam goes, or making code more testable and AI-navigable.
entries.description_zh: 设计深模块的统一词汇表。适用于设计或改进模块接口、寻找加深模块的机会、决定接缝位置，或让代码更易测试、更便于 AI 导航时。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/codebase-design folder, not the whole repo). It supplies a precise glossary (module, interface, implementation, depth, seam, adapter, leverage, locality) and principles for designing deep modules — lots of behaviour behind a small interface at a clean seam — guiding interface simplification, seam placement, and testability decisions.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/codebase-design 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它提供一套精确的词汇表（模块、接口、实现、深度、接缝、适配器、杠杆、局部性）和设计深模块的原则——即在干净的接缝处用小接口承载大量行为——用于指导接口精简、接缝定位和可测试性决策。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill is reference vocabulary and design guidance that shapes how the agent reasons about and discusses module design; it has no inherent external side effects. Any code or doc changes happen only when you apply its recommendations through other tools, and it may read CONTEXT.md and ADRs to align terminology.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能是一套参考词汇与设计指引，用于塑造 agent 推理和讨论模块设计的方式，本身没有外部副作用。只有当你通过其他工具采纳其建议时才会产生代码或文档变更，它还可能读取 CONTEXT.md 和 ADR 以对齐术语。

entries.name: Diagnosing Bugs
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: A disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions; use when something is broken, throwing, failing, or slow.
entries.description_zh: 针对疑难 bug 和性能退化的严谨诊断循环；当出现报错、崩溃、失败或变慢时使用。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/diagnosing-bugs folder, not the whole repo). It walks the agent through a phased diagnosis discipline — first build a tight, red-capable feedback loop (failing test, curl, CLI, replay, bisection, etc.), then reproduce and minimise, hypothesise, instrument, fix, and add a regression test — refusing to theorise before a reproducing signal exists.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/diagnosing-bugs 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它引导 agent 走一套分阶段的诊断纪律——先构建一个紧凑、能变红的反馈循环（失败的测试、curl、CLI、回放、二分等），再复现并最小化、提出假设、加埋点、修复并补上回归测试——在出现可复现信号之前拒绝凭空推理。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill actively runs code against your project: it writes and executes failing tests, curl/HTTP scripts, CLI invocations, headless-browser drivers, and may add temporary instrumentation. As a last resort it can request access to a reproducing environment or production instrumentation. Review what it runs; commands execute in your repo with your credentials and environment.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能会主动在你的项目上运行代码：它会编写并执行失败的测试、curl/HTTP 脚本、CLI 调用、无头浏览器驱动，并可能添加临时埋点。作为最后手段，它可能请求访问可复现的环境或生产环境埋点。请审查它运行的内容；这些命令会在你的仓库中以你的凭据和环境执行。

entries.name: Domain Modeling
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Build and sharpen a project's domain model. Use when pinning down domain terminology or a ubiquitous language, recording an architectural decision, or when another skill needs to maintain the domain model.
entries.description_zh: 构建并打磨项目的领域模型。适用于厘清领域术语或统一语言、记录架构决策、或其他技能需要维护领域模型时。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/domain-modeling folder, not the whole repo). It actively challenges terms, sharpens fuzzy language into canonical terms, stress-tests relationships with concrete edge-case scenarios, cross-checks claims against the code, and writes the glossary into CONTEXT.md (or per-context files via CONTEXT-MAP.md) and decisions into docs/adr/ as they crystallize.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/domain-modeling 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它会主动挑战术语、把模糊用语收敛成规范术语、用具体的边界场景压测概念间的关系、把陈述与代码相互印证，并在术语和决策成形时把术语表写入 CONTEXT.md（或经由 CONTEXT-MAP.md 写入各上下文文件）、把决策写入 docs/adr/。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill creates and edits documentation files in your repo — CONTEXT.md, CONTEXT-MAP.md, and ADRs under docs/adr/ — and reads your source to cross-check claims. It is otherwise conversational with no external side effects; the main effect is new and modified docs in your working tree.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能会在你的仓库中创建和编辑文档文件——CONTEXT.md、CONTEXT-MAP.md 以及 docs/adr/ 下的 ADR——并会读取你的源码以相互印证陈述。除此之外它以对话为主、无外部副作用；主要影响是在工作区中新增和修改文档。

entries.name: Grill Me
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: A relentless interview that stress-tests a plan or design until you reach shared understanding.
entries.description_zh: 用一场穷追不舍的访谈来压力测试你的方案或设计，直到双方达成共识。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/productivity/grill-me folder, not the whole repo). It is a user-invoked entry point that kicks off a grilling session: the agent interviews you one question at a time about every branch of your plan or design, recommending an answer for each, until the whole decision tree is resolved.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/productivity/grill-me 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它是一个由用户手动触发的入口，启动一场“烤问”会话：智能体就你方案或设计的每一个分支逐条提问，每次只问一个问题并给出推荐答案，直到整棵决策树被逐一厘清。
entries.sec_note_en: Pure conversational orchestration: it only drives a question-and-answer interview and makes no external changes (no files written, no commands run, no network access). The agent may read your codebase to answer questions it can resolve itself.
entries.sec_note_zh: 纯对话式编排：它只驱动一问一答的访谈，不做任何外部改动（不写文件、不执行命令、不联网）。智能体可能会读取你的代码库来自行解答某些问题。

entries.name: Grill With Docs
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: A relentless interview that sharpens a plan or design while producing docs (ADRs and a glossary) as you go.
entries.description_zh: 一场刨根问底的访谈，在打磨方案或设计的同时随手产出文档（ADR 和术语表）。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/grill-with-docs folder, not the whole repo). It runs a stateful grilling session against your existing domain model, interrogating a plan one question at a time to expose gaps, and crystallises decisions inline by updating CONTEXT.md, ADRs, and the project glossary as terms get sharpened.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/grill-with-docs 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它针对你既有的领域模型运行一场有状态的拷问会话，逐个问题地审讯方案以暴露盲点，并在术语被打磨清晰的同时就地落定决策——更新 CONTEXT.md、ADR 和项目术语表。
entries.sec_note_en: Mostly a conversational planning skill, but it does write to your repo: it creates and edits CONTEXT.md, ADR files, and a glossary as decisions land. It also depends on the sibling grilling and domain-modeling skills being available. No code execution, but review the doc changes it commits to your working tree.
entries.sec_note_zh: 它主要是一个对话式的规划技能，但确实会写入你的仓库：随着决策落定，它会创建并编辑 CONTEXT.md、ADR 文件和术语表。它还依赖同仓的 grilling 与 domain-modeling 技能可用。不执行代码，但请审查它写入工作区的文档改动。

entries.name: Grilling
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design to stress-test it before building.
entries.description_zh: 在动手开发前，就方案或设计对用户穷追不舍地提问，以充分压力测试。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/productivity/grilling folder, not the whole repo). It walks down each branch of the design tree one decision at a time, asking a single question per turn and recommending an answer, resolving dependencies between decisions until you reach shared understanding; it prefers exploring the codebase over asking when a question can be answered there.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/productivity/grilling 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它沿着设计决策树逐个分支推进，每轮只问一个问题并给出推荐答案，逐一厘清决策之间的依赖关系，直到达成共识；当某个问题能通过代码库回答时，它会优先去探查代码库而非直接发问。
entries.sec_note_en: Pure conversational orchestration: it only drives a question-and-answer interview and makes no external changes (no files written, no commands run, no network access). It is model-invokable, so the agent may trigger it on grill-style phrasing; it may read your codebase to answer questions itself.
entries.sec_note_zh: 纯对话式编排：它只驱动一问一答的访谈，不做任何外部改动（不写文件、不执行命令、不联网）。该技能可被模型自动调用，因此智能体可能在出现“烤问”类措辞时自动触发；它可能会读取你的代码库来自行解答问题。

entries.name: Handoff
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document so a fresh agent can pick up the work.
entries.description_zh: 把当前对话浓缩成一份交接文档，让一个全新的智能体能够接手继续工作。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/productivity/handoff folder, not the whole repo). It writes a markdown handoff document summarising the current conversation, including a suggested-skills section, while referencing existing artifacts (PRDs, plans, ADRs, commits, diffs) by path or URL instead of duplicating them.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/productivity/handoff 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它会写出一份 Markdown 交接文档，概括当前对话并附带一个“建议技能”小节；对已有产物（PRD、计划、ADR、提交、diff）则以路径或 URL 引用，而不重复其内容。
entries.sec_note_en: Side effect: it writes one markdown handoff file to your operating system's temporary directory (not the current workspace). It instructs the agent to redact secrets and PII before writing, but you should still review the generated document before sharing it.
entries.sec_note_zh: 副作用：它会向你操作系统的临时目录（而非当前工作区）写入一份 Markdown 交接文件。它会要求智能体在写入前对密钥和个人身份信息进行脱敏，但在分享前你仍应自行复核生成的文档。

entries.name: Improve Codebase Architecture
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Scan a codebase for deepening opportunities, present them as a visual HTML report, then grill through whichever one you pick.
entries.description_zh: 扫描代码库寻找可深化的改进点，以可视化 HTML 报告呈现，再就你选定的那一项逐步拷问。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/improve-codebase-architecture folder, not the whole repo). It uses an explore agent to find architectural friction and shallow modules (guided by CONTEXT.md and ADRs), renders the candidate refactors as a self-contained Tailwind+Mermaid HTML report with before/after diagrams, and then runs a grilling loop over whichever deepening opportunity you choose.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/improve-codebase-architecture 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它用一个探索 agent 找出架构摩擦点和浅模块（以 CONTEXT.md 和 ADR 为指引），把候选重构渲染成一份自包含的 Tailwind+Mermaid HTML 报告（含前后对比图），随后就你选定的那个深化机会运行拷问循环。
entries.sec_note_en: Read-and-analyse plus reporting: it reads your codebase and writes a timestamped HTML report to the OS temp dir (not your repo) and opens it in a browser. It loads Tailwind and Mermaid from CDNs, so report rendering needs network access. The grilling phase may then update CONTEXT.md and ADRs in your repo. It depends on the sibling codebase-design, grilling, and domain-modeling skills.
entries.sec_note_zh: 以读取分析加出报告为主：它会读取你的代码库，并把带时间戳的 HTML 报告写入操作系统临时目录（不写入你的仓库），然后在浏览器中打开。它从 CDN 加载 Tailwind 与 Mermaid，因此报告渲染需要网络访问。随后的拷问阶段可能会更新你仓库中的 CONTEXT.md 和 ADR。它依赖同仓的 codebase-design、grilling 与 domain-modeling 技能。

entries.name: Prototype
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Build a throwaway prototype to flesh out a design — a runnable terminal app for state and business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route.
entries.description_zh: 构建一个用完即弃的原型来打磨设计——针对状态与业务逻辑问题做一个可运行的终端小程序，或在同一路由下生成几种风格迥异、可切换的 UI 变体。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/prototype folder, not the whole repo). It picks one of two branches — a tiny interactive terminal app to stress-test a state machine or business logic, or several radically different UI variations switchable via a URL search param — building clearly-marked throwaway code with one run command, in-memory state, no polish, to be deleted or absorbed once it answers its question.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/prototype 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它会在两个分支中择一：要么做一个小型交互式终端程序来压测状态机或业务逻辑，要么生成几种通过 URL 查询参数切换的、风格迥异的 UI 变体；产出的是明确标记为用完即弃、只需一条命令运行、内存内保存状态、不做打磨的代码，待回答完问题后即删除或吸收进正式代码。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill scaffolds and runs throwaway code in your repo — a terminal app or extra UI routes — using your existing task runner, so it executes code locally and adds temporary files near where they're used. It avoids persistence by default; if the question involves a database it may hit a clearly-named scratch DB or local file. The prototype is meant to be deleted afterward.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能会在你的仓库中搭建并运行用完即弃的代码——一个终端程序或额外的 UI 路由——使用你现有的任务运行器，因此会在本地执行代码，并在使用处附近添加临时文件。它默认不做持久化；若问题涉及数据库，可能会使用命名清晰的临时数据库或本地文件。该原型用后应被删除。

entries.name: Setup Matt Pocock Skills
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Configure this repo for the engineering skills — issue tracker, triage label vocabulary, and domain doc layout. Run once before first use.
entries.description_zh: 为本仓库配置这套工程技能——issue 追踪器、分诊标签词表和领域文档布局。首次使用其他技能前运行一次。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/setup-matt-pocock-skills folder, not the whole repo). It scaffolds the per-repo configuration the other engineering skills assume: it explores the repo (git remotes, CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, CONTEXT.md, docs/adr), walks you through choosing an issue tracker (GitHub/GitLab/local-markdown/other), maps the five triage label strings, and confirms the domain-doc layout before writing the config.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/setup-matt-pocock-skills 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它为其他工程技能搭好每个仓库所需的配置：探查仓库（git 远端、CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md、CONTEXT.md、docs/adr），引导你选择 issue 追踪器（GitHub/GitLab/本地 markdown/其他），映射五个分诊标签字符串，并在写入配置前确认领域文档布局。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill writes configuration into your repo: it adds an '## Agent skills' block to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md and records tracker/label/doc choices (e.g. under docs/agents/). It inspects git config and remotes to suggest defaults but is prompt-driven and confirms with you before writing. No code execution; review the files it adds to your working tree.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能会把配置写入你的仓库：它会向 CLAUDE.md 或 AGENTS.md 添加一个 '## Agent skills' 区块，并记录追踪器/标签/文档的选择（例如放在 docs/agents/ 下）。它会查看 git 配置和远端以建议默认值，但整个过程由提示驱动，并在写入前与你确认。不执行代码；请审查它写入工作区的文件。

entries.name: TDD
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Test-driven development. Use when building features or fixing bugs test-first, when red-green-refactor is mentioned, or when you want integration tests.
entries.description_zh: 测试驱动开发。适用于以测试先行的方式构建功能或修复 bug、提到红-绿-重构循环、或希望编写集成测试时。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/tdd folder, not the whole repo). It drives a strict red-green-refactor loop with vertical 'tracer bullet' slices (one test then one implementation, repeat) and favors integration-style tests that verify behavior through public interfaces rather than implementation details.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/tdd 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它驱动严格的红-绿-重构循环，采用纵向「曳光弹」切片（先一个测试再一个实现，循环往复），并偏好通过公共接口验证行为、而非耦合实现细节的集成式测试。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill writes and runs tests against your codebase using your project's existing test framework and task runner, so it executes code locally. It reads CONTEXT.md and ADRs (if present) to align vocabulary but does not transmit anything externally; risk is limited to local test execution.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能会基于你项目现有的测试框架和任务运行器编写并运行针对代码库的测试，因此会在本地执行代码。它会读取 CONTEXT.md 和 ADR（若存在）以对齐术语，但不会向外部传输任何内容；风险仅限于本地测试执行。

entries.name: Teach
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Teach you a new skill or concept across sessions, turning the current workspace into a stateful learning space.
entries.description_zh: 跨多次会话教你一项新技能或新概念，把当前工作区变成一个有状态的学习空间。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/productivity/teach folder, not the whole repo). It treats the current directory as a teaching workspace, grounding lessons in a MISSION.md and curated resources, and produces short, beautiful self-contained HTML lessons plus reference sheets and learning records, using retrieval practice, spacing, and interleaving to build long-term retention.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/productivity/teach 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它把当前目录当作教学工作区，以 MISSION.md 和精选资源为依据，产出简短且精美的自包含 HTML 课程，以及参考速查表和学习记录，并运用回忆练习、间隔重复与交错练习来构建长期记忆。
entries.sec_note_en: Side effect: it creates and updates files in your current directory (MISSION.md, RESOURCES.md, NOTES.md, and HTML lessons/reference/learning-record folders) as learning state, and may run a CLI command to open a lesson. Point it at a dedicated learning folder rather than a code repo. It fetches external resources to ground lessons, so it reaches the network.
entries.sec_note_zh: 副作用：它会在当前目录中创建并更新文件（MISSION.md、RESOURCES.md、NOTES.md 以及 HTML 课程/参考/学习记录等目录）作为学习状态，还可能运行 CLI 命令来打开课程。建议把它指向一个专门的学习目录，而非代码仓库。它会抓取外部资源来支撑教学，因此会访问网络。

entries.name: To Issues
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on your project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices.
entries.description_zh: 用曳光弹式纵向切片，把计划、规格或 PRD 拆解成可被独立认领的 issue，并发布到你项目的 issue 追踪器。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/to-issues folder, not the whole repo). It explores the codebase, breaks a plan into thin vertical 'tracer bullet' slices that each cut end-to-end through every layer, quizzes you on granularity and dependencies, then publishes the approved slices as issues in dependency order.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/to-issues 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它会探查代码库，把计划拆成贯穿各层、端到端的薄「曳光弹」纵向切片，就粒度与依赖关系向你确认，然后按依赖顺序把已批准的切片作为 issue 发布。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill creates issues on your configured project issue tracker (GitHub, Linear, or local files) and applies triage labels, so it needs write access to that tracker and will publish content there. It also reads your codebase, CONTEXT.md, and ADRs to phrase issues; setup of the tracker vocabulary is a prerequisite.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能会在你配置的项目 issue 追踪器（GitHub、Linear 或本地文件）上创建 issue 并打上分诊标签，因此需要对该追踪器的写入权限，并会在其中发布内容。它还会读取你的代码库、CONTEXT.md 和 ADR 来撰写 issue；预先配置好追踪器术语是前提条件。

entries.name: To PRD
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Turn the current conversation into a PRD and publish it to your project issue tracker — no interview, just synthesis of what you've already discussed.
entries.description_zh: 把当前对话直接整理成一份 PRD 并发布到你项目的 issue 追踪器——不做访谈，只综合你们已经讨论过的内容。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/to-prd folder, not the whole repo). It synthesizes the current conversation and codebase understanding into a PRD (problem statement, extensive user stories, implementation and testing decisions, preferring a single high test seam), confirms the seams with you, then publishes the document to your issue tracker with a ready-for-agent label.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/to-prd 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它把当前对话与对代码库的理解综合成一份 PRD（问题陈述、详尽的用户故事、实现与测试决策，并优先选用单一的高层测试接缝），与你确认这些接缝后，再把文档发布到你的 issue 追踪器并打上 ready-for-agent 标签。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill publishes a PRD to your configured project issue tracker (GitHub, Linear, or local files) and applies a triage label, so it needs write access there and will create content. It reads your codebase, CONTEXT.md, and ADRs to draft the PRD; tracker vocabulary must be set up beforehand.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能会把一份 PRD 发布到你配置的项目 issue 追踪器（GitHub、Linear 或本地文件）并打上分诊标签，因此需要对其的写入权限，并会创建内容。它会读取你的代码库、CONTEXT.md 和 ADR 来起草 PRD；需事先配置好追踪器术语。

entries.name: Triage
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: Move issues and external PRs through a state machine of triage roles — categorise, verify, grill if needed, and write agent-ready briefs.
entries.description_zh: 用一套分诊角色状态机来流转 issue 和外部 PR——分类、验证、必要时拷问，并写出可交给 agent 的工作简报。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/engineering/triage folder, not the whole repo). It moves incoming issues (and optionally external PRs) through canonical triage roles — categorising bug vs enhancement, assigning states like needs-triage / needs-info / ready-for-agent, verifying the claim by reproducing it, grilling underspecified requests into shape, and posting agent-ready briefs back to the tracker.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/engineering/triage 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它把进来的 issue（以及可选的外部 PR）按规范化的分诊角色流转——区分 bug 与增强、打上 needs-triage / needs-info / ready-for-agent 等状态、通过复现来验证主张、把描述不清的请求拷问成形，并把可交给 agent 的简报回贴到追踪器。
entries.sec_note_en: This skill reads from and writes to your issue tracker (GitHub via the gh CLI by default, or GitLab/local-markdown/other), so it needs that tracker's access and will create labels, post comments, and change issue/PR states. It expects setup-matt-pocock-skills to have configured the label vocabulary first. When verifying claims it may check out PR branches and run their tests/commands — review code it executes.
entries.sec_note_zh: 该技能会读写你的 issue 追踪器（默认通过 gh CLI 使用 GitHub，也支持 GitLab/本地 markdown/其他），因此需要该追踪器的访问权限，并会创建标签、发表评论、变更 issue/PR 状态。它要求先用 setup-matt-pocock-skills 配置好标签词表。在验证主张时，它可能检出 PR 分支并运行其测试/命令——请审查它执行的代码。

entries.name: Writing Great Skills
entries.author: mattpocock
entries.description_en: A reference of the vocabulary and principles that make an agent skill predictable, for writing and editing skills well.
entries.description_zh: 一份关于词汇与原则的参考，讲解如何让智能体技能更可预测，用于把技能写好、改好。
entries.long_en: One skill from Matt Pocock's skills collection (this entry installs only the skills/productivity/writing-great-skills folder, not the whole repo). It is a meta-skill: a pure reference that defines the levers behind predictability — invocation modes, description writing, the information hierarchy and progressive disclosure, when to split skills, pruning to a single source of truth, and using leading words — with a bundled GLOSSARY.md for the full definitions.
entries.long_zh: Matt Pocock 技能合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/productivity/writing-great-skills 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它是一个元技能：一份纯参考资料，阐明决定“可预测性”的各项杠杆——调用方式、描述撰写、信息层级与渐进式披露、何时拆分技能、剪枝至单一事实来源，以及善用“引导词”——并附带一份 GLOSSARY.md 提供完整定义。
entries.sec_note_en: Pure reference material consulted on demand: it makes no external changes (no files written, no commands run, no network access). It only shapes how you author other skills.
entries.sec_note_zh: 按需查阅的纯参考资料：它不做任何外部改动（不写文件、不执行命令、不联网）。它只影响你如何撰写其他技能。

entries.name: Web Design
entries.author: xiaopu-ai
entries.description_en: A two-phase web visual-design skill: first produce a standardized DESIGN.md spec from your PRD / reference URL / screenshot / keywords, then generate UI/UX, visual, motion, and responsive web code that meets it.
entries.description_zh: 两阶段的 Web 视觉设计技能:先从 PRD / 参考 URL / 截图 / 关键词产出标准化的 DESIGN.md 规范,确认后据此生成达标的 UI/UX、视觉、动效与响应式网页代码。
entries.long_en: Specializes in web surfaces—landing pages, portfolios, product pages, blogs, personal sites, SaaS pages—gating code generation behind an agreed design spec.
entries.long_zh: 专攻 web 端——落地页、作品集、产品页、博客、个人站、SaaS 介绍页——先定规范再出代码。
entries.sec_note_en: Generates local web code and assets; review output before deploying.
entries.sec_note_zh: 生成本地网页代码与素材;部署前请审阅产物。

entries.name: Lanshu Animated Diagram
entries.author: cclank
entries.description_en: Turn an article or system design into a premium black-canvas hand-drawn architecture/process diagram from one JSON spec, producing an editable .excalidraw source, a static PNG preview, and a genuinely animated GIF with moving flow highlights and pulsing module accents. Renders locally with Python/Pillow using a fixed art-directed layout, and ships frame-diff verification to prove the GIF actually moves.
entries.description_zh: 用一份 JSON spec 把文章或系统设计整理成高质感的黑底手绘架构/流程图（岚叔动态架构图风格），同时产出可编辑的 .excalidraw 源文件、静态 PNG 预览，以及带流动高亮与模块脉冲的真正动图 GIF。基于固定的美术排版用本地 Python/Pillow 渲染，并自带逐帧差分校验来证明 GIF 确实在动。
entries.long_en: Converts technical content into a consistent, polished diagram set (Excalidraw + PNG + animated GIF) so architecture and process explanations read as designed visuals rather than flat boxes-and-arrows.
entries.long_zh: 把技术内容转成统一、精致的一套图（Excalidraw + PNG + 动图 GIF），让架构与流程讲解呈现为有设计感的视觉，而非平铺的方框与箭头。
entries.sec_note_en: Runs a bundled Python renderer (Pillow) entirely on the local machine to emit .excalidraw/.png/.gif — no network, remote APIs, or external icon libraries. Requires Python 3.9+ and Pillow (ffprobe optional for media inspection). Authored and marketed as a Codex skill, but uses the portable SKILL.md format and a platform-agnostic Python renderer.
entries.sec_note_zh: 在本地运行内置的 Python 渲染器（Pillow）生成 .excalidraw/.png/.gif——不联网、无远程 API、无外部图标库。需要 Python 3.9+ 与 Pillow（ffprobe 可选，用于媒体检查）。作者按 Codex skill 定位与宣传，但采用通用的 SKILL.md 格式与平台无关的 Python 渲染器。

entries.name: HyperFrames
entries.author: heygen-com
entries.description_en: HyperFrames by HeyGen is an open-source Claude Code plugin that turns HTML/CSS/JS compositions into deterministic MP4 videos, bundling 19 on-demand skills—a router plus creation workflows (product launch, website-to-video, faceless explainer, PR-to-video, captions, motion graphics, music, slideshow) and domain skills (core composition contract, animation adapters, creative direction, media, CLI) that teach agents the full video-production loop.
entries.description_zh: HeyGen 出品的 HyperFrames 是一个开源 Claude Code 插件,把 HTML/CSS/JS 合成稿渲染成可复现的 MP4 视频,内置 19 个按需加载的技能——一个路由技能,加上创作工作流(产品发布、网站转视频、无人脸讲解、PR 转视频、字幕、动态图形、音乐、幻灯片)与领域技能(核心合成契约、动画适配器、创意方向、媒体、CLI),教 agent 完成完整的视频制作流程。
entries.long_en: Write HTML, render video: an Apache-2.0, agent-native framework whose renderer seeks each frame in headless Chrome and encodes with FFmpeg for reproducible output—no React, no build step, no proprietary timeline, and no per-render fees.
entries.long_zh: 写 HTML,渲染视频:一个 Apache-2.0、面向 agent 的框架,渲染器在无头 Chrome 中逐帧 seek 并用 FFmpeg 编码,产出可复现——无需 React、无需构建步骤、无私有时间轴,也没有按次渲染收费。
entries.sec_note_en: Standalone local renderer, NOT a paid-API shell. Requires Node.js 22+, FFmpeg on PATH, and headless Chrome driven via Puppeteer (the @hyperframes/engine package); renders run locally with an optional AWS Lambda path. It reads your composition HTML/media and writes MP4s into the project. Optional media features (TTS voiceover, background music/SFX) can use a HeyGen/ElevenLabs/Gemini API key, but fall back to fully local models (Kokoro TTS, MusicGen, Whisper, u2net background-removal) that pull large model downloads (~75MB–3GB) and need Python—none are required for HTML-to-video rendering. Cloning the full repo also fetches ~240MB of Git LFS golden-test baselines (skippable with GIT_LFS_SKIP_SMUDGE=1).
entries.sec_note_zh: 独立本地渲染器,而非付费 API 薄壳。需要 Node.js 22+、PATH 上的 FFmpeg,以及经 Puppeteer 驱动的无头 Chrome(@hyperframes/engine 包);渲染默认在本地运行,可选 AWS Lambda 路径。它读取你的合成 HTML/媒体,把 MP4 写入项目。可选的媒体功能(TTS 配音、背景音乐/音效)可使用 HeyGen/ElevenLabs/Gemini 的 API key,但也能回退到完全本地的模型(Kokoro TTS、MusicGen、Whisper、u2net 抠图),这些会下载较大的模型文件(约 75MB–3GB)并依赖 Python——HTML 转视频的渲染本身并不需要它们。克隆完整仓库还会拉取约 240MB 的 Git LFS 基准测试产物(可用 GIT_LFS_SKIP_SMUDGE=1 跳过)。

entries.name: FFmpeg Video Analyzer
entries.author: fabriqaai
entries.description_en: Analyzes a local video file by extracting frames with ffmpeg/ffprobe and delegating frame reading to disposable vision sub-agents, then synthesizes a timestamped, step-by-step Markdown summary (timeline, key moments, narrative).
entries.description_zh: 用 ffmpeg/ffprobe 从本地视频抽帧，把读帧交给一次性的视觉子代理，再综合成带时间戳的逐步 Markdown 摘要（时间线、关键时刻、叙述）。
entries.long_en: A context-efficient pipeline—frame images live only inside sub-agent contexts while the main agent reads text-only batch reports—so understanding screen recordings, tutorials, presentations, or footage costs roughly 90% less context than reading frames directly.
entries.long_zh: 上下文高效的流水线——帧图像只存在于子代理上下文中，主代理只读纯文本批次报告——理解屏幕录制、教程、演示或素材时，比直接读帧省约 90% 上下文。
entries.sec_note_en: Requires ffmpeg and ffprobe installed locally and an agent with an AI vision model to read frames; reads the given video and writes JPEG frames plus Markdown to a temp dir (cleaned up afterward). The repo has no LICENSE file committed (the README states MIT, but no license is actually present).
entries.sec_note_zh: 需要本地安装 ffmpeg 与 ffprobe，并需一个具备 AI 视觉模型的代理来读帧；会读取给定视频、向临时目录写入 JPEG 帧与 Markdown（完成后清理）。仓库未提交 LICENSE 文件（README 写的是 MIT，但实际并未附带许可证）。

entries.name: Remotion Best Practices
entries.author: remotion-dev
entries.description_en: Domain knowledge and best practices for building videos as code with Remotion (React)—animating via useCurrentFrame()/interpolate(), composition and sequence setup, captions, effects, audio, and a one-frame render check.
entries.description_zh: 用 Remotion（React）以代码方式制作视频的领域知识与最佳实践——通过 useCurrentFrame()/interpolate() 做动画、组合与时序编排、字幕、特效、音频，以及单帧渲染自检。
entries.long_en: Loads Remotion-specific rules (40+ topic files on layout, transitions, fonts, 3D, audio, ffmpeg, voiceover and more) so an agent writes renderable Remotion code instead of CSS/Tailwind animation patterns that silently fail to render.
entries.long_zh: 加载 Remotion 专属规则（40+ 个主题文件，涵盖布局、转场、字体、3D、音频、ffmpeg、配音等），让代理写出可渲染的 Remotion 代码，而非那些会静默渲染失败的 CSS/Tailwind 动画写法。
entries.sec_note_en: A knowledge/best-practices skill that scaffolds and renders real Remotion projects via npx (create-video, remotion studio/render), so it needs Node.js plus React and the Remotion toolchain. Remotion ships under its own license (free for individuals and small teams, paid for larger companies), which is not a standard OSS license—check it before commercial use. The skills repo itself has no LICENSE file at root.
entries.sec_note_zh: 这是知识/最佳实践型技能，会通过 npx 实际脚手架并渲染 Remotion 项目（create-video、remotion studio/render），因此需要 Node.js 以及 React 与 Remotion 工具链。Remotion 使用其自有许可证（个人与小团队免费、较大公司需付费），并非标准开源许可证——商用前请先查看。skills 仓库本身根目录没有 LICENSE 文件。

entries.name: Video Use
entries.author: browser-use
entries.description_en: Edit any video by conversation with a coding agent—transcribe, cut filler and dead space, color grade, generate overlay animations, and burn subtitles—reasoning from the raw transcript plus on-demand timeline visuals instead of frame-dumping.
entries.description_zh: 用编码代理通过对话编辑任意视频——转写、剪掉口头语与空白、调色、生成叠加动画、烧录字幕——以原始转录文本加按需的时间线可视化为依据，而非整片抽帧。
entries.long_en: Ask → confirm → execute → self-eval → persist: it reasons over a packed phrase-level transcript, snaps every cut to word boundaries, applies 30ms audio fades, composites overlays (PIL/Remotion/HyperFrames/Manim) and subtitles, then checks its own rendered output at each cut before showing you.
entries.long_zh: 提问 → 确认 → 执行 → 自检 → 持久化：基于打包的短语级转录推理，把每个剪切点对齐到词边界，加 30ms 音频淡入淡出，合成叠加动画（PIL/Remotion/HyperFrames/Manim）与字幕，再在展示前对自己渲染的输出逐个剪切点自检。
entries.sec_note_en: Needs ffmpeg/ffprobe and Python (uv/pip) deps installed locally; transcription uses the hosted ElevenLabs Scribe API and requires an ELEVENLABS_API_KEY (a paid cloud service) stored in a .env at the repo root. Optional Node.js for HyperFrames/Remotion overlays, Manim for diagram slots, and yt-dlp for online sources. Reads your footage and writes all outputs to <videos_dir>/edit/. MIT licensed.
entries.sec_note_zh: 需要本地安装 ffmpeg/ffprobe 与 Python（uv/pip）依赖；转写使用托管的 ElevenLabs Scribe API，需要 ELEVENLABS_API_KEY（付费云服务），存放在仓库根目录的 .env 中。叠加动画可选 Node.js（HyperFrames/Remotion）、图解片段需 Manim、在线源需 yt-dlp。会读取你的素材并把全部输出写入 <videos_dir>/edit/。MIT 许可证。

entries.name: Manim Composer
entries.author: adithya-s-k
entries.description_en: Turn a vague explainer-video idea into a detailed, scene-by-scene plan (scenes.md): researches the topic, asks about audience/scope/style, then specifies mobjects, animations, narration, pacing, and a color palette in 3Blue1Brown style—before any code is written.
entries.description_zh: 把模糊的讲解视频想法变成逐场景的详细方案（scenes.md）：先研究主题，再询问受众/范围/风格，然后以 3Blue1Brown 风格写清每场的对象、动画、旁白、节奏与配色——在写任何代码之前完成。
entries.long_en: One skill from adithya-s-k's manim_skill collection (this entry installs only the skills/manim-composer folder, not the whole repo). It is the planning stage, version-agnostic: it produces a scenes.md spec (narrative hook, scene breakdowns, transitions, color palette, math content, implementation order) meant to be handed off to manimce-best-practices or manimgl-best-practices for actual implementation. Ships reference docs on 3b1b narrative patterns and visual techniques plus a blank scenes template; it plans and does not render.
entries.long_zh: adithya-s-k 的 manim_skill 合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/manim-composer 这一个目录，而非整仓）。它是与版本无关的规划阶段：产出一份 scenes.md 方案（叙事钩子、分场拆解、转场、配色、数学内容、实现顺序），交给 manimce-best-practices 或 manimgl-best-practices 去真正实现。附带 3b1b 叙事模式与视觉技巧的参考文档及空白 scenes 模板；它只做规划，不做渲染。
entries.sec_note_en: Planning-only: outputs a Markdown scenes.md and does not render video, so it needs no Manim/LaTeX/ffmpeg itself. It uses web search to research the topic, so it sends your topic to the agent's search tool. To later build the planned video you will need Python plus Manim (and a LaTeX/ffmpeg toolchain for rendering) via the companion skills.
entries.sec_note_zh: 仅做规划：输出 Markdown 版 scenes.md，不渲染视频，因此自身不需要 Manim/LaTeX/ffmpeg。它会用网络搜索研究主题，故会把你的主题发给代理的搜索工具。后续真正实现所规划的视频，需通过配套技能用到 Python 与 Manim（渲染还需 LaTeX/ffmpeg 工具链）。

entries.name: Manim CE Best Practices
entries.author: adithya-s-k
entries.description_en: Best practices for Manim Community Edition (the `from manim import *` / `manim` CLI engine): scene structure, mobjects, animations, LaTeX/MathTex, coordinate systems and graphing, 3D with ThreeDScene, camera control, updaters, styling, and CLI render flags.
entries.description_zh: Manim 社区版（`from manim import *` 与 `manim` 命令行引擎）的最佳实践：场景结构、对象、动画、LaTeX/MathTex、坐标系与绘图、ThreeDScene 三维、相机控制、updater、样式与命令行渲染参数。
entries.long_en: One skill from adithya-s-k's manim_skill collection (this entry installs only the skills/manimce-best-practices folder, not the whole repo). Targets the community-maintained ManimCE, NOT 3b1b's ManimGL. Bundles ~25 topic rule files, five tested example scripts (basic animations, math visualization, updater patterns, graph plotting, 3D), copy-and-modify scene templates, and a quick reference with render flags, Jupyter %%manim cell magic, install steps, and a ManimCE-vs-ManimGL difference table to avoid version confusion.
entries.long_zh: adithya-s-k 的 manim_skill 合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/manimce-best-practices 这一个目录，而非整仓）。面向社区维护的 ManimCE，而非 3b1b 的 ManimGL。打包约 25 个主题规则文件、五个经过测试的示例脚本（基础动画、数学可视化、updater 模式、函数绘图、三维）、可复制修改的场景模板，以及一份包含渲染参数、Jupyter %%manim 单元魔法、安装步骤与 ManimCE/ManimGL 差异对照表的速查表，以避免版本混淆。
entries.sec_note_en: Reference/best-practices skill (rule docs + example .py files); it writes nothing on its own. To actually render the examples you need Python plus Manim Community (`pip install manim`) and a local LaTeX/ffmpeg toolchain (TeX Live/MiKTeX/MacTeX + ffmpeg) for math typesetting and video encoding. All rendering is local; no paid or cloud API required.
entries.sec_note_zh: 参考/最佳实践型技能（规则文档 + 示例 .py 文件），自身不写入任何东西。要真正渲染示例，需要 Python 与 Manim 社区版（`pip install manim`），以及本地的 LaTeX/ffmpeg 工具链（TeX Live/MiKTeX/MacTeX + ffmpeg）用于数学排版与视频编码。全部本地渲染，无需付费或云端 API。

entries.name: ManimGL Best Practices
entries.author: adithya-s-k
entries.description_en: Best practices for ManimGL (3Blue1Brown's OpenGL engine, `from manimlib import *` / `manimgl` CLI): InteractiveScene, Tex with tex_to_color_map, self.frame camera control, 3D, and the interactive `-se` / checkpoint_paste() development workflow.
entries.description_zh: ManimGL（3Blue1Brown 的 OpenGL 引擎，`from manimlib import *` 与 `manimgl` 命令行）的最佳实践：InteractiveScene、带 tex_to_color_map 的 Tex、self.frame 相机控制、三维，以及 `-se` 交互模式与 checkpoint_paste() 开发流。
entries.long_en: One skill from adithya-s-k's manim_skill collection (this entry installs only the skills/manimgl-best-practices folder, not the whole repo). Targets Grant Sanderson's ManimGL, NOT Manim Community Edition. Covers ManimGL-specific patterns (ShowCreation vs Create, Tex raw strings, self.frame.reorient(), self.embed(), backstroke) with rule files, five example scripts, scene templates, and a difference table. Note the bundled example code is adapted from 3Blue1Brown's videos repo and carries its own license.
entries.long_zh: adithya-s-k 的 manim_skill 合集中的一个技能（本条目只安装 skills/manimgl-best-practices 这一个目录，而非整仓）。面向 Grant Sanderson 的 ManimGL，而非 Manim 社区版。覆盖 ManimGL 专有写法（ShowCreation 而非 Create、Tex 原始字符串、self.frame.reorient()、self.embed()、backstroke），含规则文件、五个示例脚本、场景模板与差异对照表。注意：附带示例代码改编自 3Blue1Brown 的 videos 仓库，带有其自身许可。
entries.sec_note_en: Reference/best-practices skill (rule docs + example .py files); it writes nothing on its own. To render you need Python plus ManimGL (`pip install manimgl`) and a local LaTeX/ffmpeg toolchain; ManimGL also needs OpenGL and, on Apple Silicon, brew pkg-config/cairo. Licensing caveat: bundled example code is adapted from 3Blue1Brown's videos repo under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (attribution required, NonCommercial, ShareAlike)—not for commercial use. All rendering is local; no paid or cloud API required.
entries.sec_note_zh: 参考/最佳实践型技能（规则文档 + 示例 .py 文件），自身不写入任何东西。渲染需要 Python 与 ManimGL（`pip install manimgl`）以及本地 LaTeX/ffmpeg 工具链；ManimGL 还需要 OpenGL，在 Apple Silicon 上需 brew 安装 pkg-config/cairo。许可注意：附带示例代码改编自 3Blue1Brown 的 videos 仓库，采用 CC BY-NC-SA 4.0（需署名、禁商用、相同方式共享）——不可用于商业用途。全部本地渲染，无需付费或云端 API。

entries.name: Algorithmic Art
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Generates interactive algorithmic/generative art using p5.js, producing self-contained HTML artifacts with seeded randomness and parameter controls.
entries.description_zh: 使用 p5.js 生成交互式算法/生成艺术，产出含种子随机性与参数控制的独立 HTML 产物。
entries.long_en: Creates original generative art by first drafting an algorithmic philosophy (a computational aesthetic manifesto), then implementing it as a self-contained HTML artifact. Uses p5.js loaded from the cdnjs CDN for rendering. Supports seeded randomness for reproducible outputs, parameter sliders for exploration, and seed navigation. Bundled templates (viewer.html, generator_template.js) provide an Anthropic-branded UI shell. SKILL.md explicitly instructs avoiding copying real artists' work to prevent copyright issues.
entries.long_zh: 先起草算法哲学（计算美学宣言），再将其实现为独立 HTML 产物，生成原创生成艺术。通过 cdnjs CDN 加载 p5.js 进行渲染，支持种子随机性（可复现输出）、参数滑块与种子导航。内置模板（viewer.html、generator_template.js）提供 Anthropic 品牌 UI 框架。SKILL.md 明确要求避免复制真实艺术家作品以规避版权问题。
entries.sec_note_en: Proprietary: © Anthropic, PBC (per-skill LICENSE.txt) — use is governed by your Anthropic agreement; copying outside Anthropic's Services, derivative works, and third-party redistribution are explicitly prohibited. Produced HTML artifacts fetch p5.js from cdnjs CDN at runtime — requires outbound network access in the browser. No server-side code execution; all rendering happens in the user's browser. No external API calls or credential requirements.
entries.sec_note_zh: 专有许可 © Anthropic, PBC（各技能目录内 LICENSE.txt）：使用受你与 Anthropic 的协议约束；明确禁止在 Anthropic Services 之外保留副本、创建衍生作品或向第三方转分发。生成的 HTML 产物在浏览器中运行时从 cdnjs CDN 加载 p5.js，需要出站网络访问；无服务端代码执行，渲染全在用户浏览器端完成，无外部 API 调用或凭据要求。

entries.name: Brand Guidelines
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography (Poppins/Lora) to artifacts such as slide decks and documents.
entries.description_zh: 将 Anthropic 官方品牌色彩与字体（Poppins/Lora）应用于幻灯片、文档等产物。
entries.long_en: Provides Anthropic's exact brand palette and font pairings for consistent visual styling. Main colors: Dark (#141413), Light (#faf9f5), Mid Gray (#b0aea5); accent colors: Orange (#d97757), Blue (#6a9bcc), Green (#788c5d). Applies Poppins to headings (24pt+) and Lora to body text, with Arial/Georgia fallbacks. Uses python-pptx for PPTX files. Accent colors cycle through orange, blue, and green for non-text shapes. No network access required; relies on system-installed fonts.
entries.long_zh: 提供 Anthropic 精确品牌色板与字体搭配，用于统一视觉风格。主色包括 Dark (#141413)、Light (#faf9f5)、Mid Gray (#b0aea5)；强调色为 Orange (#d97757)、Blue (#6a9bcc)、Green (#788c5d)。标题（24pt+）用 Poppins，正文用 Lora，备用 Arial/Georgia。PPTX 文件通过 python-pptx 处理。非文字形状循环使用强调色。无需网络访问，依赖系统已安装字体。
entries.sec_note_en: Proprietary: © Anthropic, PBC (per-skill LICENSE.txt) — use is governed by your Anthropic agreement; copying outside Anthropic's Services, derivative works, and third-party redistribution are explicitly prohibited. Requires Python with python-pptx installed. No network access or external API calls. Font availability depends on the system environment; falls back to Arial/Georgia automatically.
entries.sec_note_zh: 专有许可 © Anthropic, PBC（各技能目录内 LICENSE.txt）：使用受你与 Anthropic 的协议约束；明确禁止在 Anthropic Services 之外保留副本、创建衍生作品或向第三方转分发。需安装 Python 及 python-pptx。无网络访问或外部 API 调用。字体可用性取决于系统环境，自动回退至 Arial/Georgia。

entries.name: Canvas Design
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Creates original museum-quality visual art as PNG or PDF files, guided by a generated design philosophy manifesto.
entries.description_zh: 依据生成的设计哲学宣言，创作博物馆品质的原创视觉艺术，输出 PNG 或 PDF 文件。
entries.long_en: Two-phase skill: first generates a visual philosophy manifesto (named aesthetic movement, 4-6 paragraphs), then renders it as a single-page PNG or PDF using canvas drawing. Searches a bundled canvas-fonts directory for typography and may download additional fonts at runtime. SKILL.md stresses expert craftsmanship, minimal text, and original work — explicitly prohibiting copying real artists to avoid copyright issues. Multi-page output (e.g. coffee-table book style) is supported on request.
entries.long_zh: 两阶段技能：先生成视觉哲学宣言（命名美学运动，4-6 段），再通过画布绘制渲染为单页 PNG 或 PDF。从内置 canvas-fonts 目录搜索字体，运行时也可下载额外字体。SKILL.md 强调工艺感、极简文字与原创作品，明确禁止复制真实艺术家作品以规避版权问题。按需支持多页输出（如咖啡桌书风格）。
entries.sec_note_en: Proprietary: © Anthropic, PBC (per-skill LICENSE.txt) — use is governed by your Anthropic agreement; copying outside Anthropic's Services, derivative works, and third-party redistribution are explicitly prohibited. SKILL.md instructs downloading fonts at runtime if system fonts are insufficient — requires outbound network access in that case. No external API calls or credential requirements. Output files are local PNG/PDF only.
entries.sec_note_zh: 专有许可 © Anthropic, PBC（各技能目录内 LICENSE.txt）：使用受你与 Anthropic 的协议约束；明确禁止在 Anthropic Services 之外保留副本、创建衍生作品或向第三方转分发。SKILL.md 指示在系统字体不足时于运行时下载字体，该情况需出站网络访问。无外部 API 调用或凭据要求，输出文件仅为本地 PNG/PDF。

entries.name: Claude API Reference
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Reference documentation for the Claude API and Anthropic SDK — model IDs, pricing, streaming, tool use, MCP, caching, and token counting.
entries.description_zh: Claude API 与 Anthropic SDK 的参考文档，涵盖模型 ID、定价、流式传输、工具调用、MCP、缓存与 token 计数。
entries.long_en: A reference skill that loads up-to-date Claude API and Anthropic SDK documentation before answering questions about LLM-powered applications. Triggers automatically when a prompt mentions Claude/Anthropic model names, asks about pricing or rate limits, or involves agent/MCP/tool-use patterns. Covers Python and TypeScript SDKs. Designed to prevent stale-training-data mistakes; skips gracefully when a non-Anthropic provider is in use.
entries.long_zh: 一个参考型技能，在回答 LLM 应用相关问题之前自动加载最新的 Claude API 与 Anthropic SDK 文档。当提示中涉及 Claude/Anthropic 模型名称、定价限制或 agent/MCP/工具调用模式时自动触发。涵盖 Python 和 TypeScript SDK，避免因训练数据过时而产生错误答案；当检测到非 Anthropic 提供商时自动跳过。

entries.name: Doc Co-Authoring
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Guides users through a 3-stage co-authoring workflow—context gathering, section-by-section refinement, and reader testing with a fresh Claude—for docs, specs, and proposals.
entries.description_zh: 通过三阶段结构化流程（情境采集、逐节精炼、新会话读者测试）协助用户共同撰写文档、规范与提案。
entries.long_en: The skill walks you through three structured stages. Stage 1 (Context Gathering) collects background, audience, constraints, and related docs—Claude asks clarifying questions until it has enough context. Stage 2 (Refinement & Structure) builds each section through brainstorming, curation, and iterative edits, optionally using an artifact or file as the working draft. Stage 3 (Reader Testing) uses a fresh sub-agent (no context bleed) to verify the finished document answers real reader questions without blind spots. Primarily a methodology skill—no external service calls unless the user optionally enables Slack, Google Drive, or other MCP integrations.
entries.long_zh: 技能引导用户经历三个结构化阶段。第一阶段（情境采集）通过提问收集背景、受众、约束与相关资料，直至 Claude 掌握足够上下文。第二阶段（精炼与结构化）逐节通过头脑风暴、筛选与迭代修改构建文档，可选用 artifact 或本地文件作草稿。第三阶段（读者测试）启用不含当前上下文的新子 agent，验证成品文档能否令读者无歧义地理解。本质上是方法论技能——除非用户主动启用 Slack、Google Drive 等 MCP 集成，否则不调用外部服务。
entries.sec_note_en: Proprietary: © Anthropic, PBC (per-skill LICENSE.txt) — use is governed by your Anthropic agreement; copying outside Anthropic's Services, derivative works, and third-party redistribution are explicitly prohibited. Runs locally; no code execution. Pure methodology—follows a structured conversational workflow with no external service calls by default. Optional integrations (Slack, Google Drive, SharePoint) are user-configured and not bundled.

entries.name: DOCX
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Create, read, and edit Word (.docx) documents — new docs via the `docx` npm library, existing docs via an unpack-edit-repack XML workflow.
entries.description_zh: 创建、读取和编辑 Word (.docx) 文件——新文档使用 `docx` npm 库生成，已有文档通过解压-编辑 XML-重新打包工作流处理。
entries.long_en: Supports the full .docx lifecycle: read content via pandoc or raw XML, create new documents with the docx npm package (headings, tables, images, footnotes, TOC, multi-column layouts, tracked changes), and edit existing files by unpacking the ZIP/XML structure, applying changes, and repacking. Bundled Python scripts handle unpacking, packing, validation, comment injection, and tracked changes. LibreOffice is required for .doc→.docx conversion and PDF/image export. Install from `skills/docx`, then run `npm install -g docx` and ensure pandoc and LibreOffice are available.
entries.long_zh: 支持 .docx 文件的完整生命周期：通过 pandoc 或原始 XML 读取内容；使用 docx npm 包创建新文档（标题、表格、图片、脚注、目录、多栏布局、修订追踪）；通过解压 ZIP/XML、修改后重新打包来编辑已有文件。内置 Python 脚本处理解压、打包、验证、批注注入和修订追踪。.doc→.docx 转换及 PDF/图片导出需要 LibreOffice。从 `skills/docx` 安装技能，然后执行 `npm install -g docx`，并确保系统中已安装 pandoc 和 LibreOffice。

Dependencies: docx (npm), pandoc, LibreOffice, Poppler (pdftoppm). All operations are local file processing; no network calls at runtime. Bundled scripts invoke LibreOffice and pandoc as local subprocesses and manipulate the filesystem.

entries.name: Frontend Design
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Opinionated design guidance for creating distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with deliberate palette, typography, and layout choices.
entries.description_zh: 为前端界面提供有主见的设计指导，强调独特的色板、字体排印与布局，避免模板化外观。
entries.long_en: A design-lead skill that approaches every interface brief with the intent to produce a visual identity that cannot be mistaken for anyone else's. Instructs the model to brainstorm a token system (color, type, layout, signature element), critique against generic defaults, then build to the refined plan. Covers typography pairing, motion, copy writing, restraint, and accessibility basics (responsive, keyboard focus, reduced-motion). No framework is mandated — guidance is technology-agnostic.
entries.long_zh: 该技能以设计主管的视角处理每个界面需求，致力于打造无法与他人混淆的视觉风格。指导模型先规划色彩/字体/布局/标志性元素，再对照通用默认值进行批判性审查，最后按修订方案构建。涵盖字体搭配、动效、文案写作、克制原则及无障碍基础。不限定技术框架，适用于各类前端场景。

Supported formats: 3P updates (Progress/Plans/Problems), company newsletters, FAQ responses, and a general-purpose template for any other internal communication. entries.name: MCP Builder
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Step-by-step guide for building high-quality MCP servers in TypeScript or Python, covering tool design, testing with MCP Inspector, and evaluation.
entries.description_zh: 构建高质量 MCP 服务器的分步指南，支持 TypeScript 与 Python，涵盖工具设计、MCP Inspector 测试及评估。
entries.long_en: A four-phase workflow skill for creating MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers: research and planning, implementation, code review and testing, and evaluation creation. Recommends TypeScript with stateless streamable HTTP for remote servers. Loads live MCP protocol docs and SDK READMEs via WebFetch during use. Bundles bundled reference files for Python (FastMCP) and Node.js patterns, best practices, and quality checklists. Guides creation of 10-question evaluations to validate the finished server.

entries.name: PDF
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Extract text/tables, merge, split, rotate, watermark, create, fill forms, OCR, and encrypt PDF files using Python libraries and CLI tools.
entries.description_zh: 使用 Python 库和命令行工具对 PDF 文件进行文本/表格提取、合并、拆分、旋转、水印添加、创建、表单填写、OCR 及加密。
entries.long_en: Covers the full range of PDF operations: extract text with pypdf or pdfplumber (including table-to-DataFrame extraction), create multi-page PDFs with reportlab, merge/split/rotate/watermark with pypdf or the qpdf CLI, OCR scanned pages with pytesseract + pdf2image, and fill PDF forms (separate FORMS.md guide). A REFERENCE.md covers advanced pypdfium2 and pdf-lib (JavaScript) usage. All processing is local. Install from `skills/pdf`; core Python packages: pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab; optional: pytesseract, pdf2image, pandas. CLI tools: qpdf, poppler-utils (pdftotext, pdfimages), pdftk.
entries.long_zh: 涵盖完整的 PDF 操作范围：使用 pypdf 或 pdfplumber 提取文本（含表格转 DataFrame）；使用 reportlab 创建多页 PDF；使用 pypdf 或 qpdf CLI 合并、拆分、旋转、添加水印；使用 pytesseract + pdf2image 对扫描页面进行 OCR；以及填写 PDF 表单（见独立的 FORMS.md）。REFERENCE.md 中涵盖 pypdfium2 和 pdf-lib（JavaScript）的高级用法。从 `skills/pdf` 安装；核心 Python 包：pypdf、pdfplumber、reportlab；可选：pytesseract、pdf2image、pandas；CLI 工具：qpdf、poppler-utils、pdftk。

No network access at runtime; all operations are local file processing. Password-handling and encryption features operate on local files only.
entries.sec_note_zh: 专有许可 © Anthropic, PBC（各技能目录内 LICENSE.txt）：使用受你与 Anthropic 的协议约束；明确禁止在 Anthropic Services 之外保留副本、创建衍生作品或向第三方转分发。依赖项：pypdf、pdfplumber、reportlab（pip）；可选：pytesseract、pdf2image、pandas；CLI 工具：qpdf、poppler-utils、pdftk。运行时不访问网络，所有操作均为本地文件处理。密码处理和加密功能仅操作本地文件。

entries.name: PPTX
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Read, create, and edit PowerPoint (.pptx) presentations — text extraction via markitdown, creation via pptxgenjs, editing via XML unpack/repack.
entries.description_zh: 读取、创建和编辑 PowerPoint (.pptx) 演示文稿——通过 markitdown 提取文本，使用 pptxgenjs 从零创建，通过 XML 解压/重打包编辑已有文件。

entries.name: Slack GIF Creator
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Creates animated GIFs optimized for Slack (emoji 128×128 or message 480×480) using Python and PIL drawing primitives.
entries.description_zh: 使用 Python 与 PIL 绘图原语，创建针对 Slack 优化的动画 GIF（表情 128×128 或消息 480×480）。

entries.name: Web Artifacts Builder
entries.author: anthropics
entries.description_en: Builds elaborate multi-component HTML artifacts using React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui, bundled into a single self-contained file.
